Simon’s Public NAO Notebook

Triturator Analysis

Author

Simon Grimm

Published

March 28, 2024

Introduction

In Q1 we collected several pieces of information to better understand the composition of Project Runway triturator samples. One important piece was information on inbound flights to Boston, which included flight duration, airline, plane type, etc. This data was collated by a scraper created by Lenni, found here. A script that downloads the data from AWS can be found here.

We need to know where the waste on these flights ends up. We thus spent some time reaching out to a lot of different airlines and ground handling companies (see here for call notes). We’ve got information on the triturator usage for most airlines (though we have more trust in some pieces of information than in others).

Setup

Code 
# Importing packages
import seaborn as sns
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import datetime as dt
import ast
from collections import defaultdict
from IPython.display import Markdown
import random
from tabulate import tabulate
import math


def return_airline_trit_dict():
    airline_trit_dict = defaultdict(str)
    with open("data/2024-03-27_airlines_triturators.tsv") as infile:
        lines = infile.readlines()
        lines = lines[1:]
        for line in lines:

            airline, triturator, ground_handling, source = line.split("\t")
            prettier_triturator = {
                "Swissport": "Swissport\nTriturator",
                "American Airlines": "American Airlines\nTriturator",
                "Unknown": "Unknown",
                "None": "No Triturator used",
            }
            airline = airline.strip()
            triturator = triturator.strip()
            triturator = prettier_triturator[triturator]
            airline_trit_dict[airline] = triturator
    return airline_trit_dict


def return_flights():
    airline_trit_dict = return_airline_trit_dict()
    unmatched_airlines = defaultdict(int)
    with open("all_flights.tsv") as infile:
        lines = infile.readlines()
        us_flights = []
        lines = lines[1:]
        for line in lines:
            (
                origin,
                origin_code,
                date,
                terminal,
                equipment,
                flight,
                airline,
                nation,
                state,
                flight_time,
            ) = line.split("\t")
            airlines = ast.literal_eval(airline)
            prime_airline = airlines[0]
            prime_airline = prime_airline.strip()
            if nation == "Canada":
                triturator_status = "Unknown"
            elif prime_airline in airline_trit_dict:
                triturator_status = airline_trit_dict[prime_airline]
            elif (
                prime_airline == "JetBlue"
            ):  # Sometimes it's JetBlue, sometimes it's JetBlue Airways
                triturator_status = "Swissport\nTriturator"

            elif nation != "United States":
                triturator_status = "Swissport\nTriturator"
                unmatched_airlines[prime_airline] += 1

            else:
                triturator_status = "Unknown"
                unmatched_airlines[prime_airline] += 1
            us_flights.append(
                [
                    origin,
                    origin_code,
                    date,
                    terminal,
                    equipment,
                    flight,
                    prime_airline,
                    nation,
                    state,
                    flight_time,
                    triturator_status,
                ]
            )

        us_flights_df = pd.DataFrame(us_flights)

        us_flights_df.columns = [
            "Origin",
            "Origin Code",
            "Date",
            "Terminal",
            "Equipment",
            "Flight",
            "Prime Airline",
            "Nation",
            "State",
            "Flight Time",
            "Triturator Status",
        ]
        return us_flights_df


def origin_to_nation_dict():
    df = return_flights()
    df["Plotting Origin"] = np.where(
        df["Nation"] == "United States",
        df["State"],
        df["Nation"],
    )
    nation_dict = df.set_index("Plotting Origin")["Nation"].to_dict()
    return nation_dict


def time_to_float(time):
    time = time.strip()
    try:
        time_object = dt.datetime.strptime(time, "%H:%M:%S")
        hours = time_object.hour + time_object.minute / 60 + time_object.second / 3600
        return hours
    except Exception as e:
        print(f"conversion of time {time} failed: {e}")
        return 0


def get_first_date_and_last_date():
    df = return_flights()

    first_date = df["Date"].min()

    last_date = df["Date"].max()
    last_date = pd.to_datetime(last_date, format="%B %d, %Y")

    last_date = last_date.strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
    return first_date, last_date
Code 
from IPython.display import Markdown
from tabulate import tabulate

table = pd.read_csv("data/2024-03-27_airlines_triturators.tsv", sep="\t")[
    ["Airline", "Triturator", "Ground handling", "Source"]
]
Markdown(
    tabulate(
        table.values,
        headers=["Airline", "Triturator", "Ground Handling", "Source"],
        showindex=False,
    )
)
Airline Triturator Assignments {#cell-airline_triturators}
Airline Triturator Ground Handling Source
Delta Air Lines Swissport nan Email by Keith Serkiz (Swissport)
JetBlue Airways Swissport nan Email by Keith Serkiz (Swissport)
American Airlines American Airlines Unknown Email by Keith Serkiz(Swissport)
United Airlines American Airlines Unknown Email by Keith Serkiz (Swissport)
Southwest Airlines Swissport nan Email by Keith Serkiz (Swissport)
Porter Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
LATAM Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
Hawaiian Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
BermudAir Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
Korean Air Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
Iberia Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
Fly Play Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
SAS Scandinavian Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
Qatar Airways Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
Qatar Executive Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
TAP Air Portugal Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
Turkish Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
Hainan Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
El Al Israel Airlines Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
Aer Lingus Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
ITA Airways Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
Condor Swissport Swissport Email by Michael Kisko (Swissport)
British Airways Unknown Dnata Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata), Call with IAG
Cape Air nan nan Call with Cape Air representative
Emirates Swissport Dnata Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata)
Lufthansa Swissport Dnata Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata)
Spirit Airlines Unknown ATS Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata)
Alaska Unknown Unknown No source
Icelandair Swissport Triangle Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata)
Allegiance Swissport Triangle Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata)
Air France Swissport Triangle Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata)
Copa Airlines Swissport Triangle Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata)
Cathay Pacific Swissport Dnata Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata)
Swiss Swissport Dnata Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata)
Japan Airlines Swissport Dnata Call with Michael McPherson (Dnata)

Flights across Triturators

Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = return_flights()

    nicer_trit_labels = {
        "aa_triturator": "American Airlines \nTriturator",
        "pr_triturator": "Swissport\nTriturator",
        "unknown": "Unknown",
    }
    df = df.replace({"Triturator Status": nicer_trit_labels})

    # create a df with number of flights per triturator
    df = df.groupby("Triturator Status").size().reset_index(name="Flights")
    # sort the df by number of flights
    df = df.sort_values(by="Flights", ascending=False)
    return df


def return_destination_trit_plot():
    df = return_plotting_df()

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 3))

    # sns colors (tab). no trit = orange, unknown = red swissport = green, american airlines = blue
    colors = {
        "No Triturator used": "tab:orange",
        "Unknown": "tab:red",
        "Swissport\nTriturator": "tab:green",
        "American Airlines\nTriturator": "tab:blue",
    }

    df.plot(
        kind="barh",
        x="Triturator Status",
        y="Flights",
        ax=ax,
        legend=False,
        color=[colors[i] for i in df["Triturator Status"]],
    )

    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flights")
    plt.title("Total Flights per Triturator")

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig("triturator_flights.png", dpi=600)


return_destination_trit_plot()

Airlines

Flights per Airline

Here are flights per airline that arrive at each triturator.

Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = return_flights()
    df = (
        df.groupby(["Prime Airline", "Triturator Status"])
        .size()
        .reset_index(name="Flights")
    )
    df = df.pivot(index="Prime Airline", columns="Triturator Status", values="Flights")
    df["Total"] = df.sum(axis=1)
    df = df.sort_values(by="Total", ascending=False)
    df = df.drop("Total", axis=1)
    return df


def return_destination_trit_plot():
    df = return_plotting_df()

    df = df.head(50)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 7))
    df.plot.barh(stacked=True, ax=ax, width=0.8)

    # drop title of legend box
    ax.legend().set_title("")
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flights")
    first_date, last_date = get_first_date_and_last_date()
    plt.title(
        f"Total Flights per Airline and Triturator (Top 50)\n{first_date} - {last_date}"
    )

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


return_destination_trit_plot()

Flight Hours per Airline

Here are flight hours per airline that arrive at each triturator.

Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = return_flights()

    df["Flight Hours"] = df["Flight Time"].apply(time_to_float)

    df = (
        df.groupby(["Prime Airline", "Triturator Status"])
        .agg({"Flight Hours": "sum"})
        .reset_index()
    )

    df = df.pivot(
        index="Prime Airline", columns="Triturator Status", values="Flight Hours"
    )

    df["Total"] = df.sum(axis=1)
    df = df.sort_values(by="Total", ascending=False)
    df = df.drop("Total", axis=1)

    return df


def return_destination_trit_plot():
    df = return_plotting_df()

    df = df.head(50)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 7))
    df.plot.barh(stacked=True, ax=ax, width=0.8)

    # drop title of legend box
    ax.legend().set_title("")
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flight Hours")
    first_date, last_date = get_first_date_and_last_date()
    plt.title(
        f"Total Flight Hours per Airline and Triturator (Top 50)\n{first_date} - {last_date}"
    )

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


def start():
    return_destination_trit_plot()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    start()

Airlines

Flights per Origin Location

Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = return_flights()

    df["Flight Hours"] = df["Flight Time"].apply(time_to_float)

    df["Plotting Origin"] = np.where(
        df["Nation"] == "United States",
        df["State"],
        df["Nation"],
    )
    # group by origin and trit status and sum flight hours
    df = (
        df.groupby(["Plotting Origin", "Triturator Status"])
        .size()
        .reset_index(name="Flights")
    )
    df = df.pivot(
        index="Plotting Origin", columns="Triturator Status", values="Flights"
    )
    df["Total"] = df.sum(axis=1)
    df = df.sort_values(by="Total", ascending=False)
    df = df.drop("Total", axis=1)

    return df


def return_destination_trit_plot():
    df = return_plotting_df()
    nation_dict = origin_to_nation_dict()

    df = df.head(50)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 7))
    df.plot.barh(stacked=True, ax=ax, width=0.8)

    for i, label in enumerate(ax.get_yticklabels()):
        label.set_size(9)
        origin = label.get_text()
        if nation_dict[origin] != "United States":
            label.set_size(9)
            label.set_weight("bold")
    ax.get_legend().set_title("")
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flights")
    first_date, last_date = get_first_date_and_last_date()
    plt.title(
        f"Total Flights per Country/State and Triturator (Top 50)\n{first_date} - {last_date}"
    )

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig("triturator_destination_flighs.png", dpi=600)


return_destination_trit_plot()

Flight Hours per Origin Location

Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = return_flights()

    df["Flight Hours"] = df["Flight Time"].apply(time_to_float)

    df["Plotting Origin"] = np.where(
        df["Nation"] == "United States",
        df["State"],
        df["Nation"],
    )

    df = (
        df.groupby(["Plotting Origin", "Triturator Status"])
        .agg({"Flight Hours": "sum"})
        .reset_index()
    )

    df = df.pivot(
        index="Plotting Origin",
        columns="Triturator Status",
        values="Flight Hours",
    )

    df["Total"] = df.sum(axis=1)
    df = df.sort_values(by="Total", ascending=False)
    df = df.drop("Total", axis=1)

    return df


def return_destination_trit_plot():
    df = return_plotting_df()
    nation_dict = origin_to_nation_dict()

    df = df.head(50)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 7))
    df.plot.barh(stacked=True, ax=ax, width=0.8, color=sns.color_palette("tab10"))

    for i, label in enumerate(ax.get_yticklabels()):
        label.set_size(9)
        origin = label.get_text()
        if nation_dict[origin] != "United States":
            label.set_weight("bold")

    ax.get_legend().set_title("")
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flight Hours")
    first_date, last_date = get_first_date_and_last_date()
    plt.title(
        f"Total Flight Hours per Country/State and Triturator (Top 50)\n{first_date} - {last_date}"
    )

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.savefig("triturator_destination_flight_hours.png", dpi=600)


return_destination_trit_plot()

Remaining airlines we are unsure about

Code 
def return_plotting_df():
    df = return_flights()

    df["Flight Hours"] = df["Flight Time"].apply(time_to_float)
    df = df[df["Triturator Status"] == "Unknown"]

    df = (
        df.groupby(["Prime Airline", "Triturator Status"])
        .agg({"Flight Hours": "sum"})
        .reset_index()
    )
    df = df[df["Flight Hours"] > 1000]

    df = df.pivot(
        index="Prime Airline", columns="Triturator Status", values="Flight Hours"
    )

    df["Total"] = df.sum(axis=1)
    df = df.sort_values(by="Total", ascending=False)
    df = df.drop("Total", axis=1)

    return df


def return_destination_trit_plot():
    df = return_plotting_df()

    df = df.head(50)
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 3))
    df.plot.barh(stacked=True, ax=ax, width=0.8)

    ax.legend().set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
    ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
    plt.tick_params(axis="y", which="both", left=False, right=False)
    plt.ylabel("")
    plt.xlabel("Total Flight Hours")
    first_date, last_date = get_first_date_and_last_date()
    plt.title(
        f"Airlines where we do not have a confident triturator assignment (>1000 flight hours)\n{first_date} - {last_date}"
    )

    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()


def start():
    return_destination_trit_plot()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    start()
